data.title: Former Treasury Secretary Jack Lew Discusses Social Security Trust Fund Outlook
data.description:
Former US Treasury Secretary Jack Lew joined Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on “Bloomberg Money.”

The latest annual Social Security Trustees report moved up the projected date when reserves run out to the fourth quarter of 2032, according to Bloomberg, and at that point the program would have enough to pay 78% of scheduled benefits.

data.title: STOCKS | Roku in Talks to Sell Itself, Sources Say
data.description: Roku Inc. is in talks to sell itself, people with knowledge of the matter said. According to Bloomberg, the discussions are ongoing and no deal is guaranteed.

data.title: Times Square Crystal Ball to Rise at U.S. Stock Market Close, Report Says
data.description:
A report said the crystal ball in New York’s Times Square will rise at the close of the U.S. stock market as a tribute to SpaceX.

According to Jin10, The Wall Street Journal cited people familiar with the matter as saying the ball will be raised when U.S. equities finish trading.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Meta Plans Tighter Controls on Employee Token Use as AI Costs Rise
data.description:
Meta Platforms said in an internal memo that it plans to tighten controls on employees’ token usage as artificial intelligence-related costs increase.

According to Jin10, The Information reported that Meta expects its internal AI usage costs to reach the tens of billions of dollars in 2026.

The memo described the move as part of efforts to manage rising expenses tied to AI operations.

data.title: Nick Colas: SpaceX Valuation Doesn’t Add Up on the Math
data.description: Nick Colas, co-founder of DataTrek Research, said SpaceX’s valuation doesn’t add up compared with the level of investment into the space technology company. According to Bloomberg, he said investors are betting on Elon Musk’s track record of success and his ability to bring interesting technology to the public, rather than on math that people can “rationally model.”

data.title: Dangote Refinery Boosts Nigeria Crude Buying as West Africa Demand Softens
data.description: Nigeria’s Dangote refinery is increasing purchases of the country’s crude, helping to offset weakening demand for West African grades amid uncertainty over the resumption of oil shipments from the Middle East, according to Bloomberg,

data.title: Chevron CEO Says Company Open to Expanding Middle East Footprint Despite Iran Conflict
data.description: Chevron Corp. is open to expanding its Middle East footprint despite the ongoing Iran conflict that has triggered an unprecedented disruption of global energy markets, according to Bloomberg, citing Chief Executive Officer Mike Wirth.

data.title: AI | SpaceX Rents Full Colossus 1 Data Center Capacity to Anthropic After Grok Setbacks
data.description: SpaceX decided to rent out the full capacity of its Colossus 1 data center in Memphis to Anthropic PBC after encountering technical challenges using the facility to develop and run its Grok artificial intelligence models. According to Bloomberg, the decision was described by people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Dogecoin Jumps Nearly 6% After SpaceX’s Historic Nasdaq IPO
data.description: Dogecoin (DOGE) surged nearly 6% on Friday, climbing toward $0.0905 as investors reacted to SpaceX’s historic IPO on Nasdaq. According to BeInCrypto, SpaceX began trading under the ticker SPCX after pricing shares at $171, raising about $75 billion and valuing the company near $1.8 trillion, with reported demand topping $350 billion. DOGE was around $0.0900 at the time of writing, up more than 6% in 24 hours and nearly 9% in seven days, but still 87.8% below its May 2021 all-time high of $0.7316.

data.title: STOCKS | Microsoft Considered Spinning Off Xbox Unit, Report Says
data.description: Microsoft previously considered separating its Xbox business to operate independently and planned to release new games in the Halo and Fallout franchises. According to Jin10, the report was published by The Information.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Survey Finds 64% of Americans Worry About AI-Driven Job Loss
data.description:
Anthropic said a survey found that 64% of Americans are worried about losing jobs due to AI. According to NS3.AI, more than 70% of respondents said the government should play a role in regulating AI.

The findings indicate concerns about AI’s impact on employment and public support for government involvement in AI oversight.

data.title: STOCKS | European Stocks End Week Near Record High as Oil Falls on US-Iran Deal Speculation
data.description: European stocks ended the week near a record high as oil fell on speculation a deal between the US and Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is near. In markets, according to Bloomberg, the move in crude supported risk sentiment across the region.

data.title: Liberland Fires Technology Secretary After Allegations of Blockchain Control Centralization
data.description:
Liberland has fired its secretary of technology, Dorian Stern Vukotic, after alleging he centralized control of the Liberland Blockchain and blocked President Vit Jedlicka from voting.

According to NS3.AI, Liberland also demanded that Vukotic return Ministry of Finance funds and transfer control of a liquidity pool within seven days.

The report did not provide additional details on the alleged centralization or the amount of funds involved.

data.title: James Chanos: SpaceX Mega IPO Is a Troubling Sign for Markets
data.description: Veteran short seller James Chanos said a SpaceX mega IPO is a troubling sign for broader markets. According to Bloomberg, Chanos said “we’re going to shatter” records this year when it comes to IPOs, speaking on “Bloomberg Money” with Tom Keene and Scarlet Fu.

data.title: Japan Lower House Passes Bill to Bring Crypto Under FIEA, Opening Path to Spot ETFs
data.description:
Japan’s lower house passed a bill that would move cryptocurrencies under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act, creating a pathway for spot crypto ETFs and introducing a flat 20% capital-gains tax.

According to NS3.AI, the reform is not yet law because the bill must still pass the upper house, where approval is widely expected.

Representatives from the Tokyo Stock Exchange said crypto ETFs could begin listing as early as 2027 once the regulatory framework is finalized.

The flat 20% capital-gains tax is expected to take effect in 2028 under Japan’s 2026 Tax Reform Outline.

data.title: Misam Abidi Indicted Over Alleged Star Credit Holdings Crypto Ponzi Scheme
data.description:
A federal indictment accuses Misam M. Abidi of running an alleged crypto Ponzi scheme through Star Credit Holdings from 2020–2024 and diverting more than $1.9 million to himself and family.

According to NS3.AI, Abidi faces 11 counts tied to the alleged scheme.

The charges include wire fraud, money laundering, operating an unlicensed money transmitting business, and filing false tax returns.

data.title: Columbia to Reinstate SAT, ACT Requirement for Admissions Starting in 2027
data.description: Columbia University will again require applicants to submit SAT or ACT scores for admission starting in 2027. According to Bloomberg, the university said a faculty review found the tests were “a useful indicator of potential student success.”

data.title: World Cup Betting Volume Could Reach $10 Billion, Bernstein Analysts Say
data.description:
The World Cup could generate up to $10 billion in betting volume across sports-betting and prediction-market platforms. According to NS3.AI, Bernstein analysts expect roughly $3 billion in additional wagers tied to the tournament.

The analysts also estimate there will be about 60% more bettable inventory than in prior tournaments, suggesting an expanded range of markets and events available for wagering.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Putin Says Russia Expanding Domestic Satellite Constellation, Vows More Strikes on Ukraine Infrastructure
data.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia is expanding a constellation of domestically produced satellites and vowed to step up strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure. According to Bloomberg, he linked the satellite expansion to broader efforts as the war continues.

data.title: Robinhood Says Platform Traffic Hit a Record High
data.description:
Robinhood said its platform traffic hit an all-time high today.

According to Jin10, the company described the traffic level as the highest in its history.
